quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. "Gadget out-earns Sprocket by 2.12x", "West trails North by 35.0%", and "a 1.54x gap" of "$761.53".

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data — e.g. "Prioritize Gadget stock... at 33.4% of revenue" and "Investigate West, which trails North by 35.0%... close the $761.53 regional revenue gap."

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations such as the "right-skewed spread" from median below mean and that Gadget "leads... despite low unit counts, driven by its $149.50 price" versus Widget scaling with volume.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. "Gadget out-earns Sprocket by 2.12x", "35.0% behind North", "differ by $761.53, a 1.54x gap".

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with figures, e.g. "Investigate West, which trails North by 35.0%; targeted campaigns could close the $761.53 regional revenue gap."

insight_density.why: Multiple non-obvious, actionable observations beyond totals, e.g. the right-skew ("median...below the mean...a few high-priced Gadget orders pull the average upward") and Gadget's revenue concentration "despite low unit counts."

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. 'Gadget out-earns Sprocket by 2.12x', 'West trails... by 35.0%', and 'a 1.54x gap' of $761.53.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names products/regions with figures, e.g. 'Prioritize Gadget stock... at 33.4% of revenue' and 'Investigate West, which trails North by 35.0%... close the $761.53 regional revenue gap.'

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like the 'right-skewed spread' from high-priced Gadgets and 'Gadget out-earns Sprocket by 2.12x even though Sprocket sells far more units.'
